core.label: Assembly Fundamentals
core.description: Architecture basics, registers, and instruction set.

topics.label: CPU Architecture Overview
topics.description: Understand registers, ALU, memory hierarchy, and the fetch-decode-execute cycle.

topics.label: Assembler Setup (NASM)
topics.description: Install NASM and write your first program. NASM is the most popular x86 assembler.

topics.label: Registers (x86-64)
topics.description: General purpose (rax-r15), flags, instruction pointer, stack pointer, and segment registers.

topics.label: Data Movement (MOV, LEA)
topics.description: Move data between registers and memory. MOV is the most used instruction.

topics.label: Arithmetic Instructions
topics.description: ADD, SUB, MUL, IMUL, DIV, IDIV, INC, DEC, NEG. Integer arithmetic operations.

topics.label: Control Flow (JMP, Jcc, CMP)
topics.description: Conditional and unconditional jumps, comparison, and flags. Assembly's version of if/else and loops.

topics.label: The Stack (PUSH, POP)
topics.description: Use the stack for local variables, function arguments, and saving registers.

topics.label: Functions (CALL, RET)
topics.description: Calling conventions, stack frames, prologue/epilogue. How functions work at the machine level.

topics.label: System Calls
topics.description: Invoke OS services with syscall (Linux) or int 0x80. Read, write, exit system calls.

topics.label: Data Section & Memory
topics.description: Define data with db, dw, dd, dq in .data and .bss sections. Understand memory layout.

core.label: Advanced Assembly
core.description: Addressing modes, SIMD, debugging, and linking.

topics.label: Addressing Modes
topics.description: Immediate, register, direct, indirect, indexed, and base+displacement addressing.

topics.label: Bitwise Operations
topics.description: AND, OR, XOR, NOT, SHL, SHR, ROL, ROR. Bit manipulation at the lowest level.

topics.label: String Instructions
topics.description: MOVSB, CMPSB, STOSB, LODSB, SCASB with REP prefix. Fast memory operations.

topics.label: Floating Point (SSE/AVX)
topics.description: SIMD instructions for parallel floating-point math. Modern x86 uses SSE/AVX instead of x87.

topics.label: Calling Conventions
topics.description: System V AMD64 ABI — argument passing, return values, and callee/caller-saved registers.

topics.label: Linking with C
topics.description: Link assembly with C code using gcc/ld. Call C library functions from assembly.

topics.label: Debugging with GDB
topics.description: Debug assembly with GDB — inspect registers, memory, and step through instructions.

topics.label: Godbolt Compiler Explorer
topics.description: Study compiler output and write assembly interactively in the browser.
